title: Restrict SSO domain users from being able to invite other members in the same domain name
excerpt: We have SSO with Google enabled. Currently anyone in the domain can invite others to monday.com since they are in the same domain.  I would like to to prevent this from happening for those with Member roles.  This is to …

posts.author: marc509383 (Marc Nagata)
posts.cookedHtml: We have SSO with Google enabled. Currently anyone in the domain can invite others to monday.com since they are in the same domain. I would like to to prevent this from happening for those with Member roles. This is to help with license our license control. Support informed me that since we are SSO, the Member Permission settings do not apply to users with the same domain.

posts.author: MarkP (Mark)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Marc,

I completely agree! This has been one of my biggest gripes. If someone within your organization accesses your monday.com instance using the URL (never officially invited), they’re automatically granted a license, even if you don’t have any available. Then, your account manager will notify you that you’re over your limit and offer an upgrade.

Since this process leads to customers needing more licenses, which increases monday.com’s revenue, I’m not sure this issue will ever be addressed.

posts.author: MelB (Melanie Broxis)
posts.cookedHtml: I’ve just fallen into this trap! Thought I was doing a good thing by turning on SSO and now I find people that have joined that shouldn’t have. Which means I’m going to be spending a lot of time changing people from members to viewers… So really need the ability to control licenses as in a very large Corp. like mine we have tight budgets and can’t just keep upgrading because 1 time someone logged in or suddenly got access to something they shouldn’t have…
